summary.intent: Merge the main branch into the copilot-enterprise branch, resolve any conflicts, then commit and push the result.
summary.outcome: Successfully merged main into copilot-enterprise, resolved three conflict regions in github_copilot.rs, verified compilation, and committed the merge.

repo: The copilot-enterprise branch replaced get_valid_copilot_token with get_copilot_auth, which returns an auth object with base_url and token fields.
The copilot-enterprise branch adds an enterprise streaming bridge, while main added responses API helpers — both needed to coexist.
Project uses Rust and lives under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/copilot-enterprise.

code.finding: Code after the second conflict region already uses auth.base_url and auth.token, confirming HEAD's get_copilot_auth() was the correct auth path to keep; main's bare token variable was stale.

workflow: After resolving conflicts, grep for conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) to verify none remain before compiling.
Compile check before staging/committing catches any resolution mistakes early.

friction: Three-way conflict in github_copilot.rs required careful analysis of post-conflict code context to determine which auth approach (get_copilot_auth vs get_valid_copilot_token) was authoritative.
open_items: The needs_responses_api check from main was added after the enterprise auth guard — worth verifying the ordering is correct under all code paths.
No push confirmation visible in transcript — unclear if the push to remote succeeded.
